key_insight: NOTES combines a DeepONet neural operator with CMA-ES to search a compact, topology-aware latent space for fast and transferable PDE-constrained inverse design.
engineering_relevance: This enables robust optimization of complex physical designs with far fewer degrees of freedom, making inverse design more practical for nanophotonics and structural mechanics.
method: Train a DeepONet to learn a low-dimensional latent representation of topology-informed designs.
Run CMA-ES in latent space to optimize designs under PDE constraints.
Evaluate the optimized candidates with the governing physics solver for transferability and performance.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, the method reduces the design dimension from 256 to 25 and achieves over 95% efficiency in nanophotonic beam-deflector tests, while also finding structural designs with compliance down to 246.
watch_item: The approach depends on the quality of the learned latent representation and on accurate PDE-solver evaluation, so generalization may weaken if the training distribution or physics setting shifts substantially.
paper_title: Neural Operator-enabled Topology-informed Evolutionary Strategy for PDE-Constrained Optimization
